Every day I check my email. Yesterday I checked my email.
I write a report every week. Last week I wrote a report.
I go to meetings. Yesterday I went to a meeting.
Now I am confident. On Day 1 I was nervous.
Hi Danilo! Eight lessons already. Do you remember your first day?
Yes, I remember. On my first day, I was very nervous. I only said my name.
And now? How do you feel now?
Now I am more confident. I talk about my team and my project every day.
That is great progress. What did you do yesterday in English?
Yesterday, I had a meeting, and I asked three questions.
Wonderful. Do you still make mistakes?
Yes, I make mistakes sometimes, but I practice and I improve.
Perfect. Let us compare: Lesson 1 you and Lesson 8 you. Big difference!
It is a big difference. My goal is to achieve more every week.
Now I am more confident than on my first day.
Every day I check my email and I go to meetings.
Yesterday I had a meeting and I wrote a report.
I make mistakes sometimes, but I practice and I improve.
My goal is to achieve more and to remember the words.

Hi Danilo, it's Rachel. I just want to say great job this month. I remember Lesson 1, your first day. You were a little quiet, and you said only short sentences. Now look at you! You talk about your team, your routine, and the project. You ask good questions, and you give clear answers. You make small mistakes sometimes, but that is normal. The important thing is that you practice every day and you improve. I am really happy with your progress. Keep going, and soon you will be very confident. See you in class. Bye!

Let me compare Lesson 1 and Lesson 8. In Lesson 1, I was nervous. I only said my name and my job. I did not ask questions. Now, in Lesson 8, I am more confident. I talk about my work every day. Yesterday, I had a meeting in English, and I asked three questions. Last week, I wrote a short report in English too. I still make mistakes, but I remember the words better now. My goal is to practice more and to improve every week. I think I made good progress, and I want to achieve more.
